title: Explain This Operator
conclusion: IndexerPrologQuantQuantLoop is a source-level unrolled loop that interleaves query, key and weight semantic stages, while also carrying dynamic KV-cache updates.
evidence: Semantic labels from Query-Linear through Weight-Linear
reasoning: The loop body contains multiple semantic_label switches rather than one flat operator stage.
Key-Quant writes through scatter_update, so the case is not only compute-bound but also runtime-object aware.
This makes the case suitable for demonstrating unified explanation from source intent to runtime behavior.
suggestedActions: Highlight semantic anchors in the source pane.
Jump from Query-Linear to its compiled pass snapshot.
Open the runtime summary after the semantic tour.
riskNotes: This demo card is based on a curated source slice, not full-project live parsing.

title: Trace This Path
conclusion: PATH0_6 is the cleanest first branch for the demo because it preserves the source loop identity, survives into OoOSchedule, and already has matching runtime evidence.
evidence: OoOSchedule keeps the same semantic distribution and subgraph scale
reasoning: ExpandFunction shows the branch before subgraph partitioning, with all ops still in subgraph -1.
PreGraphProcess is the structural turning point where 316 subgraphs appear.
OoOSchedule then becomes the right stage to explain execution intent without changing the branch identity.
suggestedActions: Show ExpandFunction -> PreGraphProcess -> OoOSchedule as a three-step storyboard.
Annotate top subgraphs 302/301/294/293 as the visible compiled split.
Explain that path tracing is more important than raw field browsing.
riskNotes: PATH0_6 is chosen for narrative clarity; other PATH branches are intentionally hidden in v0.1.

title: Analyze Execution Bottleneck
conclusion: The runtime story is dominated by Query-Linear volume, while Key-Linear contributes longer sampled compute spans and Weight-Linear acts as a smaller side branch.
evidence: Sampled Key-Linear event duration up to 18.64
Sampled Weight-Linear event duration around 9.04 on AIC and 2.64 on AIV
reasoning: Volume and criticality are not the same: Query-Linear is the most frequent explicit semantic stage, but Key-Linear has heavier sampled tasks.
This gives the demo a realistic Agent message: explain why the busy stage is not always the only optimization target.
Using summarized swimlane evidence keeps the demo fast and still grounded in a real trace.
suggestedActions: Render a semantic-top chart instead of the full trace first.
Allow click-through from Query-Linear and Key-Linear summaries into example tasks.
Use the right panel to frame optimization as a hypothesis, not a certainty.
riskNotes: The first demo only uses summarized runtime evidence, so it should avoid claiming exact root cause.
